addressable-fire-alarm-system.intro: An addressable fire alarm system gives each detector, call point and interface a unique address, so the panel pinpoints the exact device in alarm rather than a broad zone. Icomply design, install and commission addressable fire systems to BS 5839 for offices, factories, care premises and multi-tenant buildings across Greater Manchester and the North West. From cause-and-effect programming to battery sizing and zone chart handover, our Stockport engineers deliver clear fixed-price quotes and certification-ready paperwork.
addressable-fire-alarm-system.body: Addressable loops suit larger or higher-risk buildings where speed of location reduces false evacuation and downtime. We survey existing conventional systems for upgrade paths, integrate sounders, interfaces and networked panels where needed, and train duty holders on silence, reset and weekly testing. Planned maintenance, fault finding and logbook support keep your addressable estate audit-ready for insurers and enforcing authorities.
addressable-fire-alarm-system.meta_desc: Addressable fire alarm system design, install & service to BS 5839. Stockport, Manchester & North West. Fixed quotes from Icomply.
focus_points: BS 5839 survey, design and device addressing strategy
Loop install, programming and cause-and-effect commissioning
Zone charts, logbooks and service certificates
Upgrades from conventional systems with minimal disruption
faq: What is an addressable fire alarm system?
Each device has a unique address so the control panel shows the precise detector or call point in alarm or fault, improving response and reducing search time compared with conventional zones.
Do I need addressable instead of conventional?
It depends on building size, risk and fire strategy. We assess occupancy, travel distances and future expansion so you only pay for the category and technology that match the site.
Can Icomply maintain my existing addressable panel?
Yes. We service common commercial platforms, replace devices, clear faults and provide BS 5839-aligned inspection certificates across the North West.
addressable-fire-alarm-system.seo_keywords: addressable fire alarm system, addressable fire alarm installation, BS 5839 addressable, fire alarm North West, addressable fire alarm Manchester, addressable fire alarm Stockport, loop system install, Icomply fire alarms

conventional-fire-alarm-system.intro: Conventional fire alarm systems group detectors and call points into hard-wired zones, offering a proven, cost-effective solution for smaller commercial units, shops and simple layouts. Icomply specify, install and service conventional panels to BS 5839 from our Stockport base, covering Greater Manchester, Cheshire, Lancashire and Merseyside. You receive a transparent scope, fixed pricing after survey and full commissioning documentation for landlords and facilities managers.
conventional-fire-alarm-system.body: We match zone layouts to escape routes and compartmentation, fit MCP and detector coverage to the agreed category, and leave clear zone charts at the panel. Where growth or multi-building complexity is likely, we discuss addressable options honestly so you are not locked into the wrong technology. Ongoing servicing, battery replacement and weekly-test support keep conventional systems reliable and inspection-ready.
conventional-fire-alarm-system.meta_desc: Conventional fire alarm system install & service to BS 5839. North West coverage from Stockport. Fixed-price quotes — Icomply.
focus_points: Zone design aligned to your fire risk assessment
faq: When is a conventional fire alarm suitable?
Smaller premises with simple layouts and limited expansion often suit conventional zones. We confirm suitability against BS 5839 and your fire risk assessment.
How many zones will I need?
Zone count follows building size, floors and compartmentation. After survey we propose a layout that balances cost with clear location of alarms.
Can you upgrade conventional to addressable later?
Often yes. We plan cable routes and panel locations with future upgrades in mind where practical, and can stage works to control capital spend.
conventional-fire-alarm-system.seo_keywords: conventional fire alarm system, conventional fire alarm installation, BS 5839 conventional, fire alarm zones, conventional fire alarm Manchester, conventional fire alarm Stockport, North West fire alarms, Icomply

fire-alarm-panel-service.intro: Fire alarm panel service keeps control equipment, batteries, power supplies and indications working when you need them most. Icomply engineers inspect, clean, test and certificate panels under BS 5839 planned maintenance across Stockport, Manchester and the wider North West. Whether you run Advanced, Kentec, C-TEC, Hochiki-compatible or mixed estates, we provide fixed-price service visits, clear defect lists and logbook entries your insurer and auditor will recognise.
fire-alarm-panel-service.body: A typical panel service covers standby batteries, charger voltage, indicators, sounders, fault simulation and review of recent history. We replace ageing batteries before failure, rectify earth faults and open circuits where scope allows, and escalate major remediations with a written quote. Multi-site contracts and reactive call-outs are available for FM teams managing several buildings.
fire-alarm-panel-service.meta_desc: Fire alarm panel service & BS 5839 maintenance. Batteries, faults & certificates. Stockport & North West — Icomply.
focus_points: Scheduled panel inspection and battery checks
faq: How often should a fire alarm panel be serviced?
BS 5839 typically expects competent person inspection at least every six months, with additional weekly user tests. We tailor visit frequency to your risk assessment and insurer requirements.
What brands do you service?
We regularly work on Advanced, Kentec, C-TEC, Morley, Hochiki and Apollo device estates among others. Tell us the panel model when you enquire.
Do you provide same-week panel call-outs?
Where capacity and access allow we aim for same-week attendance, prioritising systems in fault or isolation across our North West coverage area.

Icomply survey, design, install and commission detection systems for commercial, industrial and residential blocks throughout Greater Manchester and the North West. fire-detection-system-installation.body: We select optical, heat, multi-sensor or aspirating devices to suit environments such as kitchens, workshops and clean spaces, and coordinate interface modules for plant shutdown or access control release. Installation includes containment, labelling, zone charts and user training so duty holders can complete weekly tests confidently. Post-install maintenance contracts protect your investment and compliance position.

Design and install work is aligned to BS 5839 for fire detection and fire alarm systems in non-domestic premises, plus your fire risk assessment recommendations.
How long does installation take?
Small systems may complete in days; larger multi-floor sites need phased programmes. We issue a programme after survey based on access and working hours.
Will installation disrupt my business?

smoke-detector-installation-commercial.intro: Commercial smoke detector installation must match occupancy, ceiling height, airflow and false-alarm risk—not just place devices at random. Icomply specify and fit optical, multi-sensor and specialist detectors for offices, warehouses, retail and care environments across the North West. Working to BS 5839, our engineers from Stockport survey coverage gaps, upgrade ageing heads and integrate new detectors onto addressable or conventional panels with clear certification.
smoke-detector-installation-commercial.body: We avoid common pitfalls such as detectors too close to kitchens, bathrooms or HVAC outlets that drive unwanted alarms. Heat or multi-criteria devices are recommended where smoke detectors are unsuitable. After install we test sensitivity paths, update device text and leave the logbook current so your responsible person can evidence compliance.

Optical, multi-sensor and heat device selection
False-alarm reduction advice for busy sites
faq: How many smoke detectors does my commercial building need?
Quantity and type follow BS 5839 category, room geometry and fire strategy. We calculate coverage after measuring the premises rather than guessing from floor area alone.
Can you replace detectors without changing the panel?
Often yes, provided the protocol and bases match. We check compatibility before ordering devices so you avoid wasted stock.
Do you install multi-sensor detectors?
Yes. Multi-sensor heads are widely used in commercial spaces to balance early detection with fewer nuisance alarms.

fire-alarm-call-point-installation.intro: Manual call points let staff raise the alarm early when fire is seen before automatic detection. Icomply install, replace and reposition fire alarm call points to BS 5839 guidance—typically near exits and storey exits—across commercial and care premises in Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we supply compatible MCPs, glass or resettable elements, protective covers where justified, and full testing into your panel.
fire-alarm-call-point-installation.body: Poorly sited or damaged call points undermine evacuation strategy and fail inspections. We survey travel routes, fit surface or flush units, label correctly and update zone charts. For high false-alarm risk areas we discuss hinged covers only where risk assessment supports them. Service contracts include call-point operation checks on each visit.
fire-alarm-call-point-installation.meta_desc: Fire alarm call point installation & replacement to BS 5839. North West engineers from Stockport — fixed quotes. Icomply.
focus_points: New install, replacement and wiring repairs

Panel test, labels and logbook update
faq: Where should fire alarm call points be fitted?
Generally on escape routes at exits and storey exits, at heights and spacing consistent with BS 5839 and your fire risk assessment.
Glass or resettable call points?
Both are common. Resettable units reduce glass-spares issues; we match the type already on site unless you prefer a site-wide standard.
Can you add call points to an existing system?
Yes. We confirm spare capacity on conventional zones or addressable loops and quote materials and labour as a fixed package after survey.

care-home-fire-alarm.body: We work with care managers on detector choice in bedrooms and kitchens, call-point protection, cause-and-effect for doors and plant, and clear weekly-test routines that night staff can follow. faq: What fire alarm category do care homes usually need?
Many care homes require higher coverage categories following risk assessment—often L1 or L2 for life protection. We confirm category against your FRA and building layout.
Can you work around residents during upgrades?
Yes. We plan room-by-room or wing-by-wing works, coordinate with managers, and keep means of escape protected throughout.
Do you train care staff on weekly tests?
Handover includes practical weekly-test guidance and logbook completion so the responsible person can evidence routine checks.

office-fire-alarm-installation.intro: Office fire alarm installation protects staff, visitors and business continuity in open-plan floors, meeting suites and multi-tenant blocks. office-fire-alarm-installation.body: Modern offices often need multi-sensor detection, visual alarm devices for accessibility, and interfaces to access control or HVAC. Yes. We programme with principal contractors, protect finished areas and complete cause-and-effect before handover.

factory-fire-alarm-system.intro: Factory fire alarm systems face harsh environments: dust, heat, high ceilings, process plant and shift working. Icomply design, install and maintain industrial fire detection to BS 5839 for manufacturing and warehouse-linked production sites across the North West. From Stockport our engineers specify robust devices—heat, beam, aspirating or multi-sensor—interface to plant shutdowns and deliver commissioning that production managers can trust.
factory-fire-alarm-system.body: We survey mezzanines, racking and process lines for detector suitability, plan cable routes away from damage, and stage testing so production stoppages stay controlled. Cause-and-effect can include roller shutter release, extract isolation or process interlocks where the fire strategy requires it. Service contracts include industrial call-out options and spare device holding for critical lines.

focus_points: Industrial detection selection for heat, dust and height
Robust servicing and reactive engineering support
faq: Which detectors suit factory floors?
It depends on the process. Heat, beam or aspirating systems often outperform standard optical smoke in dusty or high-ceiling spaces. We recommend after site survey.
Can the fire system shut down machinery?
Interfaces can signal plant or BMS for controlled shutdown where specified in the fire strategy. We coordinate with your electrical and process teams.
Do you cover multi-building industrial estates?
Yes. We design networked or zoned solutions and offer multi-building maintenance under one agreement.

fire-alarm-zone-chart.intro: A fire alarm zone chart is the visual map that tells responders which area a zone or device relates to—essential at the panel for firefighters and duty holders. Icomply produce, update and install accurate zone charts after new installs, alterations or panel replacements across Greater Manchester and the North West. Our Stockport team aligns chart graphics with actual zone wiring and address text so inspections no longer fail on missing or out-of-date drawings.
fire-alarm-zone-chart.body: We survey the building, confirm zone boundaries, create clear laminated or framed charts and fix them adjacent to the control panel as expected under BS 5839 practice. When you add walls, tenants or detectors we revise the chart as part of the variation works. Combined with logbook and certificate packs, correct zone charts complete a professional compliance picture.
fire-alarm-zone-chart.meta_desc: Fire alarm zone chart creation & updates to BS 5839 practice. Manchester, Stockport & North West — Icomply.
focus_points: Clear panel-side chart design and print
Part of full BS 5839 documentation packs
faq: Is a zone chart a legal requirement?
BS 5839 expects suitable zone information at the control equipment so the origin of alarms can be identified quickly. Missing or wrong charts are a common inspection defect.
Can you update charts without rewiring?
Yes. If zoning is correct we redraw and replace the chart only. If zoning is wrong we quote remedial works separately.
Do addressable systems still need charts?
Yes. Device text helps, but a clear plan at the panel remains best practice for fire crews and unfamiliar staff.

fire-alarm-fault-finding.intro: Fire alarm fault finding restores systems stuck on earth fault, open circuit, battery trouble or intermittent device errors. Icomply diagnose and clear faults on conventional and addressable panels for commercial clients across the North West, working from our Stockport base with manufacturer-aware methods. You get a clear explanation of the cause, fixed-price remedials where possible and a return-to-service test with logbook entry.
fire-alarm-fault-finding.body: We start at the panel event log, isolate loops or zones methodically, and use insulation and device tools to pinpoint damaged cable, water ingress, contaminated heads or failing power supplies. Where full repair needs parts lead time, we agree temporary measures only where safe and permitted. Reactive attendance is prioritised for sites with active faults or isolated detection.
fire-alarm-fault-finding.meta_desc: Fire alarm fault finding & repairs. Earth faults, loop issues & panel errors — North West engineers. Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Structured diagnosis on conventional and addressable systems
Cable, device and power supply repairs
Clear defect reports and fixed remedial quotes
faq: Why is my fire panel showing an earth fault?
Common causes include damaged cable, moisture in devices or junctions, and DIY cable damage. We locate the circuit and repair rather than silencing the problem.
How fast can you attend a fault?
We aim for same-week or faster where capacity allows, prioritising systems that are impaired. Share panel make, fault text and photos when you call.
Do you charge a call-out fee?
Attendance and labour are quoted transparently. Simple fixes may be completed on first visit; larger cable repairs are priced before works proceed.

l1-fire-alarm-system.intro: An L1 fire alarm system provides the highest automatic detection coverage category under BS 5839 for life protection in non-domestic buildings—detection throughout escape routes and almost all rooms and areas. Icomply design, install and maintain L1 systems for high-risk or complex premises across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we deliver category justification, device schedules, commissioning and ongoing service so your fire strategy is evidence-based, not guesswork.
l1-fire-alarm-system.body: L1 is often specified for care, large public buildings or where the fire risk assessment demands maximum early warning. We balance coverage with false-alarm management using appropriate multi-sensor technology and zoning. Installation programmes, user training and six-monthly servicing keep L1 estates performing as designed.
l1-fire-alarm-system.meta_desc: L1 fire alarm system design, install & service to BS 5839. North West coverage from Stockport — Icomply.
focus_points: BS 5839 L1 category design and justification
faq: What does L1 mean?
L1 is a life-protection category with automatic detection in almost all areas plus escape routes, offering the most complete room coverage under BS 5839 Part 1.
Is L1 always required?
No. Category follows risk assessment and building use. We never oversell L1 where L2 or L3 meets the assessed need.
Can you upgrade from L3 to L1?
Yes, by extending detection into additional rooms and reviewing sounder coverage, power supplies and panel capacity as part of a staged upgrade.
l1-fire-alarm-system.seo_keywords: L1 fire alarm system, BS 5839 L1, L1 fire detection, category L1 install, L1 fire alarm Manchester, L1 fire alarm Stockport, North West, Icomply

l2-fire-alarm-system.intro: An L2 fire alarm system protects life with detection in escape routes plus defined high-risk rooms—such as plant rooms, kitchens or storage—identified by the fire risk assessment. Icomply specify and install L2 systems to BS 5839 for commercial and residential blocks across the North West. Our Stockport engineers survey risk rooms carefully so L2 delivers targeted coverage without unnecessary devices in low-risk spaces.
l2-fire-alarm-system.body: We document which rooms are included and why, programme the panel accordingly and leave charts that match the agreed category. L2 is a frequent choice where L1 would be excessive but L3 would leave critical rooms unprotected. Maintenance visits verify detectors in both escape routes and risk rooms remain clean and responsive.
l2-fire-alarm-system.meta_desc: L2 fire alarm system install & service to BS 5839. Escape routes plus risk rooms — Manchester & North West. Icomply.
focus_points: Escape route and risk-area detection design
faq: How does L2 differ from L3?
L3 covers escape routes and rooms opening onto them; L2 adds selected high-risk rooms beyond that. Your fire risk assessment drives which rooms join the L2 set.
Who decides the risk rooms?
The responsible person and fire risk assessor lead; we implement detection that matches their documented strategy.
Can L2 be addressable or conventional?
Either technology can achieve L2 if coverage and performance meet BS 5839. We recommend based on size and future flexibility.
l2-fire-alarm-system.seo_keywords: L2 fire alarm system, BS 5839 L2, L2 fire detection, category L2 installation, L2 fire alarm Manchester, L2 fire alarm Stockport, North West, Icomply

l3-fire-alarm-system.intro: An L3 fire alarm system focuses life protection on escape routes and rooms that open onto those routes, giving early warning to support evacuation. Icomply design, install and service L3 systems to BS 5839 for offices, light commercial and many multi-occupancy buildings across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we provide honest category advice—L3 only where it fits the fire risk assessment—and fixed-price delivery with full documentation.
l3-fire-alarm-system.body: Typical L3 scopes include corridor and stair detection, rooms opening onto escapes, manual call points and adequate warning devices. We check door sets, voids and mezzanines that affect route protection, and integrate landlord systems where tenants share means of escape. Aftercare includes servicing and support for weekly tests by the duty holder.
l3-fire-alarm-system.meta_desc: L3 fire alarm system design & installation to BS 5839. North West offices & commercial sites — Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Detector and MCP layout for corridors and adjoining rooms
faq: Is L3 enough for my building?
Many lower-risk commercial buildings use L3 successfully, but higher-risk uses may need L2 or L1. We align to your fire risk assessment rather than a one-size default.
Does L3 include the whole floor plate?
Not necessarily. Rooms that do not open onto escape routes may be omitted unless the FRA requires otherwise.
Can you certificate an existing L3 system?
We inspect and service existing systems, identify gaps against the claimed category, and quote upgrades if coverage falls short.
l3-fire-alarm-system.seo_keywords: L3 fire alarm system, BS 5839 L3, L3 fire detection, category L3 install, L3 fire alarm Manchester, L3 fire alarm Stockport, North West, Icomply

category-ld2-fire-alarm.intro: Category LD2 fire alarms for dwellings protect escape routes and rooms or areas where the fire risk is judged high—such as kitchens or living rooms—under BS 5839-6 guidance for domestic premises. Icomply install and maintain LD2 systems for HMOs, flats and residential landlords across the North West. Our Stockport team selects Grade A, C or D equipment to match the property type, with clear landlord documentation and testing guidance for tenants.
category-ld2-fire-alarm.body: LD2 is commonly specified where a simple LD3 hallway-only approach is not enough. We survey storeys, loft conversions and open-plan layouts that change escape risk, fit interlinked detection where required, and commission so alarms are loud enough in bedrooms. Landlord packages cover multi-property portfolios with consistent standards and service records.
category-ld2-fire-alarm.meta_desc: Category LD2 fire alarm install for dwellings & HMOs to BS 5839-6. North West landlords — Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: BS 5839-6 LD2 design for houses, flats and HMOs
faq: What is the difference between LD2 and LD3?
LD3 covers escape routes only; LD2 adds high-risk rooms. Local housing standards and your risk assessment determine which is appropriate.
Do HMOs need LD2?
Many larger or higher-risk HMOs require enhanced coverage. We check against licensing conditions and BS 5839-6 recommendations.
Are battery-only alarms acceptable?
Grade and power supply depend on property type and risk. We specify mains-powered with backup where the standard and licensing expect it.
category-ld2-fire-alarm.seo_keywords: category LD2 fire alarm, LD2 fire alarm, BS 5839-6 LD2, HMO fire alarm LD2, LD2 install Manchester, LD2 Stockport, landlord fire detection, Icomply

category-ld3-fire-alarm.intro: Category LD3 fire alarms provide detection along the escape route of a dwelling—typically hallways and landings—under BS 5839-6. Icomply supply, install and certificate LD3 systems for landlords, agents and homeowners across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we fit interlinked smoke alarms or Grade-appropriate systems, test sound levels and leave simple tenant guidance so weekly or routine tests actually happen.
category-ld3-fire-alarm.body: LD3 suits many lower-risk single-family homes but may be insufficient for some HMOs or complex layouts. We advise honestly if LD2 or higher is wiser. category-ld3-fire-alarm.meta_desc: Category LD3 fire alarm installation to BS 5839-6. Landlords & homes across Manchester & North West — Icomply.
focus_points: Interlinked smoke alarm or Grade system install
Upgrade advice where LD2 is more appropriate
faq: Is LD3 the legal minimum for rented homes?
Requirements vary by nation and property type. In England, smoke and carbon monoxide rules apply; BS 5839-6 categories guide best practice. We install to the standard your risk and licensing demand.
Should kitchen heat alarms be included?
Heat alarms in kitchens are often recommended even when the formal category is LD3. We include them where they reduce risk and false alarms.
Can you service existing domestic alarms?
Yes. We test, replace expired units and document works for landlord compliance folders.

Based in Stockport, we turn “we think someone tests it” into a repeatable process with call-point rotation, recorded results and escalation when the system fails to sound.
fire-alarm-weekly-test-support.body: Weekly testing is a user duty, not a substitute for six-monthly engineer service—but it catches faults early. A competent person appointed by the responsible person—often facilities or a trained staff member. We train your nominees and document the method.
Do you replace the need for engineer service?
No. Weekly tests are additional to periodic inspection and servicing by a competent fire alarm company.
What if the weekly test fails?
Stop, record the fault, and call for engineer attendance. We provide priority fault-finding for clients on support agreements.

fire-alarm-logbook.intro: A fire alarm logbook records tests, faults, isolations, engineer visits and false alarms—evidence that the system is managed under BS 5839 and the Fire Safety Order. Icomply supply, set up and maintain logbooks for commercial and care sites across the North West, and complete professional entries during every service visit. fire-alarm-logbook.body: We issue site-specific logbooks at commissioning, show duty holders how to record weekly tests, and review false-alarm history on each service to target remediations. focus_points: Compliant logbook issue at install or service

Yes, if kept up to date and available at the panel or known location. Digital systems can work if accessible to inspectors and backed up.
What must be recorded?
Typically dates of tests, results, faults, isolations, engineer actions, false alarms and any modifications. We set out a simple checklist for staff.

vesda-installation.intro: VESDA installation delivers very early smoke detection through aspirating air sampling—ideal for high ceilings, clean rooms, warehouses, heritage voids and spaces where point detectors struggle. Icomply design, install and commission VESDA and compatible aspirating systems across the North West, integrating them with your main fire panel cause-and-effect. Stockport-based engineers handle pipe network design, sensitivity setup and documentation for insurers who specify aspirating detection.
vesda-installation.body: We survey airflow, room volume and dust loading to size sampling pipes and set staged alerts that warn before full fire alarm. Installation includes capillary drops, end-cap verification, transport-time testing and staff training on filters and faults. Planned maintenance keeps aspiration performance stable so early warning remains genuine, not nuisance.
vesda-installation.meta_desc: VESDA installation & aspirating smoke detection. Design, pipe networks & commission — North West. Icomply.
focus_points: Aspirating system design for high or complex spaces
Pipe network install and transport-time testing
Panel integration and staged alert programming
faq: When is VESDA better than point smoke detectors?
High voids, strong airflow, cold stores, clean environments or where earliest possible warning is critical often favour aspirating detection.
Does VESDA replace the main fire panel?
Usually it signals into the building fire alarm system. We design interfaces so alerts and fire signals behave as the fire strategy requires.
How often must VESDA units be serviced?
Follow manufacturer and BS guidance—filters and sensitivity checks are routine. We include aspirating devices on planned maintenance visits.
vesda-installation.seo_keywords: VESDA installation, VESDA fire detection, aspirating smoke detection install, VESDA North West, VESDA Manchester, VESDA Stockport, high ceiling smoke detection, Icomply

beam-detection-system.intro: A beam detection system uses projected optical beams to monitor large open volumes—warehouses, atria, sports halls and factories—where point detectors would be impractical. Icomply design, install and align reflective and end-to-end beam detectors to BS 5839 practice across the North West. From Stockport we survey mounting heights, building movement and sunlight paths so beams stay stable and false alarms stay low.
beam-detection-system.body: Correct alignment, bracket rigidity and contamination planning are everything with beams. We install, commission with obscuration tests, integrate to the fire panel and train staff on temporary isolation during dust-producing works. Service visits re-check alignment and clean optics so warehouse changes do not silently defeat detection.
beam-detection-system.meta_desc: Beam detection system install & alignment for warehouses and atria. BS 5839 — North West. Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Open-area beam design and mounting survey
Optics clean and realignment on service visits
faq: Reflective or end-to-end beams?
Reflective units need power at one end and suit many warehouses; end-to-end can suit very long ranges. We select after measuring the space.
Will forklifts set beams off?
Poor siting can. We position beams above traffic where practical and set delay/sensitivity within manufacturer limits to reduce nuisance alarms.
Can beams work with high racking?
Racking alters smoke behaviour and line of sight. We may combine beams with in-rack or aspirating options as the risk requires.
beam-detection-system.seo_keywords: beam detection system, beam smoke detector installation, warehouse beam detection, optical beam fire detection, beam detection Manchester, beam detection Stockport, North West, Icomply

fire-safety-order-compliance.intro: Fire Safety Order compliance (Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005) requires a responsible person to assess risk and implement general fire precautions—including detection and warning where needed. Icomply help duty holders close fire alarm and emergency lighting gaps that undermine Order compliance across the North West. From Stockport we translate fire risk assessment actions into designed systems, maintenance regimes and records that stand up to audits and enforcement visits.

faq: Does a fire alarm automatically make me compliant?
No. Compliance is wider than equipment. A suitable system, maintained and managed, supports your precautions but does not replace risk assessment or training.
Can you write my fire risk assessment?
We focus on systems installation and maintenance. We work from your FRA and can recommend independent assessors if you need one.
What records should I keep?
Risk assessment, system design/commissioning info, logbooks, service certificates and training records. We help keep the systems side complete.

faq: Do I need monitoring on every building?
It depends on risk, occupancy patterns and insurer or strategy requirements. Unoccupied periods often justify monitoring; we advise case by case.
What is dual-path monitoring?
Two communication routes (for example IP and radio/cellular) improve resilience if one path fails. Insurers sometimes require it.
Will monitoring stop false fire brigade calls?
Procedures and filtering policies help, but detection design and management remain the main false-alarm controls. We align signalling with your agreed response plan.

bs-5266-emergency-lighting.intro: BS 5266 emergency lighting sets the UK framework for escape route, open area and high-risk task lighting when mains power fails. Icomply design, install, test and certificate emergency lighting to BS 5266 for commercial, care and multi-occupied buildings across the North West. From Stockport our engineers deliver photometric-aware layouts, LED conversions, self-test options and the monthly/annual records responsible persons need for Fire Safety Order evidence.
bs-5266-emergency-lighting.body: We survey lux-critical points—changes of direction, stairs, exits, fire equipment and open areas—then specify maintained or non-maintained luminaires accordingly. Installation includes correct legends, spacing and central battery or self-contained autonomy as designed. Planned testing regimes catch failed batteries before an evacuation is compromised.
bs-5266-emergency-lighting.meta_desc: BS 5266 emergency lighting design, install & testing. North West compliance from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Install, LED upgrade and central systems
faq: Is BS 5266 a legal requirement?
The Fire Safety Order requires suitable emergency routes and lighting; BS 5266 is the key British Standard used to demonstrate good practice.
Maintained or non-maintained fittings?
Maintained stay on for normal lighting/signage roles; non-maintained energise on mains failure. We select per location function.
How long should emergency lights last on battery?
Typically three hours for many escape systems, but design duration follows risk and standard requirements for the premises.

Stockport engineers plan phasing so escape lighting remains available throughout the works and leave full test certification on completion.
led-emergency-lighting-conversion.body: We identify failed inverters, tired nickel batteries and yellowed legends that fail inspections, then propose LED bulkheads, exit signs and recessed conversions matched to mounting points. Self-test LED options can reduce manual test labour for large estates. Payback often comes from energy and maintenance savings as well as compliance certainty.
led-emergency-lighting-conversion.meta_desc: LED emergency lighting conversion & upgrades to BS 5266. Energy-saving North West installs — Icomply.

LED bulkhead, exit and recessed conversions

faq: Can every old fitting be converted?
Not always. Some chassis suit LED kits; others are better fully replaced. We recommend the safer, standards-compliant option per point.
Will conversion reduce my electricity bill?
Maintained LED signage and lower parasitic drain typically cut energy versus old fluorescent emergency gear—savings vary by quantity and hours.
Do you remove old batteries responsibly?
Yes. Life-expired batteries are removed and handled as part of responsible site practice during conversion.

Icomply supply, install and configure self-test and addressable emergency lighting across North West commercial estates from our Stockport base. You still need competent oversight—but self-test makes BS 5266 routines more practical for multi-storey and multi-site operators.
self-test-emergency-lighting.body: We specify stand-alone self-test heads or networked reporting systems depending on building size and FM preference. Defect response when self-test reports failure
faq: Does self-test remove all manual testing?
It reduces routine manual function tests, but responsible persons must still monitor results and arrange repairs. Periodic competent inspection remains wise.
Are self-test fittings more expensive?
Unit cost is higher, yet labour savings on large sites often justify the investment. We can compare lifecycle cost on survey.
Can self-test work on existing wiring?
Many self-contained LED self-test luminaires retrofit onto existing unswitched permanent lives. We confirm circuit topology first.

emergency-lighting-monthly-test.intro: An emergency lighting monthly test is a short function check—typically simulating mains failure briefly—to confirm luminaires energise as expected under BS 5266 user routines. Icomply train duty holders, perform monthly tests under contract, or audit your in-house process across North West sites. From Stockport we supply simple checklists, log formats and engineer backup when fittings fail to light or show battery faults.
emergency-lighting-monthly-test.body: Monthly tests catch open circuits, failed lamps/LEDs and obvious charger issues before the annual duration test. We can schedule technician visits for sites without facilities staff, or coach your team to complete and record tests correctly. Failures are quoted promptly so escape lighting is restored without drift.
emergency-lighting-monthly-test.meta_desc: Emergency lighting monthly test support & contracts. BS 5266 function checks — North West. Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Staff training or fully managed monthly visits
Quick remedial quotes for failed luminaires
faq: How long is a monthly emergency lighting test?
A short functional test—long enough to confirm operation, not a full three-hour drain. Exact method follows BS 5266 guidance and manufacturer instructions.
Can tenants do the monthly test?
If competent and trained, yes. We provide instruction and recording templates; complex systems may need technicians.
What if several lights fail the monthly test?
Record them, keep escape risk under review, and instruct repairs. We prioritise clusters of failures that impair routes.

emergency-lighting-annual-test.intro: An emergency lighting annual test (full duration test) discharges batteries for the designed autonomy—often three hours—to prove the system still meets BS 5266 performance. Icomply plan and execute annual tests for commercial and care premises across the North West, managing risk during discharge and recharging afterwards. Stockport-coordinated teams document results, identify weak batteries and certificate the estate for your compliance year.
emergency-lighting-annual-test.body: We schedule tests to avoid leaving buildings unprotected—often phased by floor or circuit—and restore mains charging promptly. Failed luminaires are listed with locations and quoted for replacement, including LED upgrade options. Combining annual testing with a service inspection maximises engineer efficiency on multi-building portfolios.
emergency-lighting-annual-test.meta_desc: Emergency lighting annual duration test to BS 5266. Phased testing & certificates — North West. Icomply.
focus_points: Phased methods to manage escape risk
faq: Will the annual test leave us in the dark afterwards?
Batteries need recharge time after a full drain. We phase tests and advise on temporary precautions so risk stays controlled.
How often is a full duration test required?
BS 5266 expects a periodic full-duration test, commonly annually, plus more frequent function tests.
Do self-test systems still need an engineer annual visit?
Self-test helps, but many duty holders still engage competent engineers to verify records, inspect fittings and close defects.
emergency-lighting-annual-test.seo_keywords: emergency lighting annual test, annual duration test emergency lighting, BS 5266 annual test, three hour emergency light test, annual test Manchester, Stockport, North West, Icomply

emergency-lighting-duration-test.intro: An emergency lighting duration test proves battery autonomy under load—the critical check that fittings remain lit for the designed period after mains failure. Icomply carry out duration testing to BS 5266 for offices, warehouses, care homes and common parts across Greater Manchester and the North West. Our Stockport engineers record pass/fail by location, replace weak batteries or luminaires, and leave clear certificates for insurers and enforcing officers.
emergency-lighting-duration-test.body: Duration testing is more than flipping a key switch: we monitor performance through the discharge window, note early failures, and manage restoration of supply. Results feed a prioritised remedial plan so capital spend targets true risk. For large sites we issue a programme and progress reports until the estate is closed out.
emergency-lighting-duration-test.meta_desc: Emergency lighting duration test to BS 5266. Full autonomy checks & remedials — North West. Icomply.
focus_points: Full autonomy / duration testing by circuit or area
faq: Is a duration test the same as the annual test?
The annual test is typically a full duration test. Additional duration tests may be needed after major battery or system changes.
What duration should we achieve?
Commonly three hours for escape lighting, but the design may specify otherwise. We test to the system’s designed duration.
Can you duration-test central battery systems?
Yes. Central battery and static inverter systems need specialist procedures; we plan load and isolation carefully with your site team.
emergency-lighting-duration-test.seo_keywords: emergency lighting duration test, battery duration test emergency lights, BS 5266 duration test, emergency lighting autonomy test, duration test Manchester, Stockport, North West, Icomply

Systems cover corridors, stairs, exits, nurse bases and high-risk task areas, with records that support CQC-related fire safety evidence and insurer expectations.
care-home-emergency-lighting.body: We phase works to protect dignity and sleep patterns, use robust LED fittings suited to 24/7 environments, and train staff on monthly function checks. Annual duration tests are scheduled with managers so staffing levels match any temporary risk. Combined fire alarm and emergency lighting packages simplify compliance for group care providers.
care-home-emergency-lighting.meta_desc: Care home emergency lighting install & BS 5266 testing. Resident-aware North West engineers — Icomply.
faq: Do bedrooms need emergency lighting?
It depends on the fire strategy and layout. Corridors and escapes always need cover; bedrooms are assessed case by case with the FRA.
Can you work quietly in occupied homes?
Yes. We coordinate with managers, limit noisy works, and keep tools and access controlled around residents.
Do you maintain both fire alarms and emergency lights?
Yes. Many care clients prefer one competent contractor for both life-safety systems.
care-home-emergency-lighting.seo_keywords: care home emergency lighting, nursing home emergency lights, BS 5266 care home, care emergency lighting testing, care home Manchester, Stockport, North West, Icomply

emergency-bulkhead-lights.intro: Emergency bulkhead lights are the workhorse luminaires of escape lighting—wall or ceiling mounted, robust and ideal for corridors, plant rooms, external exits and back-of-house areas. Icomply supply and install LED emergency bulkheads to BS 5266 across Greater Manchester and the North West, including maintained exit variants and self-test models. From Stockport we replace failed fluorescent bulkheads, correct spacing on under-lit routes and certificate the finished installation.
emergency-bulkhead-lights.body: Modern LED bulkheads offer better efficacy, longer life and clearer legends than ageing fluorescent units. We select IP ratings for external final exits, add wire guards where vandalism is a risk, and ensure unswitched permanent live supplies are correct. Bulkhead programmes for landlords can standardise spares across many blocks and simplify monthly testing.
emergency-bulkhead-lights.meta_desc: Emergency bulkhead lights supply & install. LED BS 5266 bulkheads — Manchester, Stockport & North West. Icomply.
focus_points: LED emergency bulkhead specification and install
Replacement programmes for failed fluorescent units
faq: Maintained or non-maintained bulkheads?
Exit-sign and some permanently lit locations use maintained; many escape luminaires are non-maintained. We specify per point.
Can bulkheads be self-test?
Yes. Self-test LED bulkheads are popular for reducing manual test time on large estates.
Do you fit bulkheads outdoors?
Yes, with suitable IP-rated emergency bulkheads at final exits and external escape paths as the design requires.
